title: Tiny Capacity Demo
subtitle: Small proof topic for the StudyBubble engine

nodes.label: Telemetry
nodes.definition: Raw signals collected from systems, such as CPU, memory, latency, logs, traces, or events.
nodes.whyItMatters: Telemetry is the starting material for every useful operational or capacity decision.
nodes.safeSentence: Telemetry is raw signal. The value comes from turning it into a useful decision.

note.summary: Start with what the system emits before trying to forecast or explain anything.

nodes.label: Baseline
nodes.definition: The normal operating pattern learned from historical telemetry.
nodes.whyItMatters: Without a baseline, a metric value is just a number without context.
nodes.safeSentence: Baseline tells us what normal looks like before we call something risky.

note.summary: A baseline gives context for whether current behavior is normal, unusual, or trending toward risk.

nodes.label: Forecast
nodes.definition: A practical estimate of future demand or utilization based on past behavior and current trend.
nodes.whyItMatters: Forecasting helps teams act before pressure becomes an incident or planning surprise.
nodes.safeSentence: Forecasting is decision support, not a magic prediction.

note.summary: A forecast helps answer what is likely to happen next and whether action is needed.

nodes.label: Dashboard
nodes.definition: A visual decision view that shows important signals, trends, risks, and next actions.
nodes.whyItMatters: A dashboard helps people understand the state of the system quickly without reading raw data.
nodes.safeSentence: A dashboard is not just charts. It is the decision view.

note.summary: The dashboard turns forecast and threshold information into something engineers and leaders can act on.

nodes.label: Decision
nodes.definition: The action or planning choice made from the evidence, such as monitor, tune, scale, or escalate.
nodes.whyItMatters: The purpose of the study map is not just to explain data, but to support good decisions.
nodes.safeSentence: The value of the pipeline is the decision it supports.

note.summary: A decision is where telemetry, baseline, and forecast become practical action.

nodes.label: Threshold
nodes.definition: A warning line that shows when a metric is approaching an unsafe or important limit.
nodes.whyItMatters: Thresholds help turn raw metrics into risk signals.
nodes.safeSentence: A threshold is the warning line before the cliff.

note.summary: Thresholds create the boundary that lets the system flag risk before it becomes a surprise.

nodes.label: Owner
nodes.definition: The person or team responsible for understanding, accepting, or acting on a capacity signal.
nodes.whyItMatters: A risk is not actionable until someone owns the response.
nodes.safeSentence: Owner mapping turns signal into accountability.

note.summary: The owner closes the loop between finding a risk and doing something about it.

paths.label: Telemetry to Forecast
paths.description: Raw system signals become a baseline, and the baseline supports a practical forecast.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain how raw telemetry becomes a practical forecast in 60 seconds.

paths.label: Telemetry to Decision
paths.description: Raw signals become a baseline, a forecast, a dashboard, and finally an owned decision.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain how telemetry becomes a practical decision in 60 seconds.

paths.label: Risk to Ownership
paths.description: Threshold context appears on the dashboard, and the owner turns risk into action.
paths.rehearsalPrompt: Explain why a risk signal needs an owner before it becomes action.
